title: Podman push results in "Requesting bearer token: invalid status code from registry 400 (Bad Request)"
body:
### Issue Description

When I try to use `podman` to push to an internal quay registry, I get an error 
`Requesting bearer token: invalid status code from registry 400 (Bad Request)`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  On internal quay registry, create organization, and then create repository  (quay.MYSUBDOMAIN.MYDOMAIN.com/organization_name/repository_name)
2. In repository, create a robot with write permission to repository
3. Clone GitHub repository that contains a Dockerfile
4. Enter the following commands:
```
podman build . --file deploy/Dockerfile --tag id_f431c8b --build-arg RELEASE=f431c8b
podman login -u="quay_robot_username" -p="quay_robot_password" quay.MYSUBDOMAIN.MYDOMAIN.com/organization_name/repository_name
podman push id_f431c8b  quay.MYSUBDOMAIN.MYDOMAIN.com/organization_name/repository_name/id_f431c8b
```

### Describe the results you received

Getting image source signatures
Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:0c23ed2428e1ef41fdb571789f6968dd47c95135311b1c8bf56462c98581694b at destination: Requesting bearer token: invalid status code from registry 400 (Bad Request)

### Describe the results you expected

Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256: ...
 71.41 MB / 71.41 MB [======================================================] 9s
Copying blob sha256:...
 1.21 KB / 1.21 KB [========================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:...
 3.01 KB / 3.01 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

RHEL8

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Please test with the latest version. Does it only happens on push or also when you try to pull a private image?

cc @mtrmac @vrothberg


Also if you use RHEL it is recommended to go through the Red Hat support channels instead.

> Requesting bearer token: invalid status code from registry 400 (Bad Request)

Could be related to authentication.  Can you also try `podman push --creds` and specify the credentials directly on push?

Also `podman --log-level=debug push …` might be interesting.

Sadly none of that contains the full contents of the HTTP “bad request” response, so if that contains a more specific error message, we wouldn’t see it that way.

If you are the administrator of the registry, does the server-side log contain more details about the failure?

@mtrmac @vrothberg @Luap99 

Here's the results of adding --log-level=debug and --creds after upgrading my podman version:

So that’s the very first credential request…

FWIW we have, in the past, seen Quay refuse three-element nested repo names (`quay.io/foo/bar/baz`), only two-element names were allowed. https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1180#issuecomment-880090958 .

Per that same issue, at least the public Quay instance now allows such repo names, I have no idea whether the also applies to the privately-deployed product.

So, it appears my usage of podman was incorrect.
After a few updates to my build and push commands, it ran successfully.

For my `podman build` command I changed
```
$ podman build . --file deploy/Dockerfile --tag id_c2e0243 
```
to
```
$ podman build . --file deploy/Dockerfile --tag quay.subdomain.domain.edu/organization_name/repository_name:id_c2e0243 

```
And I changed my push command from:
```
$ podman push  id_c2e0243  quay.subdomain.domain.edu/organization_name/repository_name/id_c2e0243
```
to
```
$ podman push  quay.subdomain.domain.edu/organization_name/repository_name:id_c2e0243
```
 

Essentially, I changed the image name (replacing the last `/` with a `:`) from:
`quay.subdomain.domain.edu/repository_name/repository_name/id_c2e0243`
to
`quay.subdomain.domain.edu/repository_name/repository_name:id_c2e0243`


While, the push is running successfully, the pushed image is not showing up on quay, but I assume that's a quay issue. 
That said, I'll be closing, this issue.
